raw_text: FITS GZIP decompression bomb in Pillow ### Impact Pillow did not limit the amount of GZIP-compressed data read when decoding a FITS image, making it vulnerable to decompression bomb attacks. A specially crafted FITS file could cause unbounded memory consumption, leading to denial of service (OOM crash or severe performance degradation). ### Patches The amount of data read is now limited to the necessary amount. Fixed in Pillow 12.2.0 (PR #9521). ### Workarounds Avoid Pillow >= 10.3.0, < 12.2.0 Only open [specific image formats](https://pillow.readthedocs.io/en/stable/releasenotes/8.0.0.html#image-open-add-formats-parameter), excluding FITS.
